questions.prompt: Correct diagnostic order when CPA breaks?

choices.text: Tracking → structure → targeting → creative → bidding → macro. Tracking checks first because measurement bugs masquerade as performance issues constantly — broken pixel/CAPI looks identical to demand collapse in the dashboard. After tracking is verified, check whether learning-phase reset, audience overlap, or wrong-pool structural issues are the cause before changing creative or bidding. Most problems live in layers 1-4; only after exhausting those should you consider seasonal/macro.

questions.explanation: Tracking first. Otherwise you're solving the wrong problem.

questions.prompt: Ad set has been in 'Learning' for 6 weeks straight. Why and what to do?

choices.text: Under-conversion — the ad set isn't accumulating 50+ optimization events per week, so it never exits learning phase. Causes: budget too low ($20/day with $20 CPA = 7 events/week), optimization event too narrow (Complete Registration getting 20/week), audience too small, or bid cap too tight. Fixes: consolidate budget into fewer ad sets to push each over the threshold, switch optimization to a higher-volume upstream event (InitiateCheckout instead of Purchase if Purchase is too rare), open targeting, or remove caps.

questions.explanation: Under 50 events/week = never exits learning. Consolidate or shift event.
